title: Assessing the Damage: Will Fourth Quarter Technology Spending Fall? (Post-9/11 IT Recession Letter)
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
abstract: Written October 5, 2001 -- less than a month after 9/11 -- this letter from Peter Kastner to IT supplier executives presents Aberdeen Group's analysis that Q4 2001 technology spending will decrease likely by double digits, followed by markedly lower growth in 2002 compared to historic norms. The letter describes how Aberdeen is adapting to the post-9/11 environment through five-tier conferencing technology and e-learning, and urges IT suppliers to think out of the box to emerge with market-share gains from the IT recession of 2001-2002.
importance_rationale: Rare post-9/11 real-time market analysis document from Aberdeen Group; captures immediate IT industry response and provides prescient assessment of the 2001-2002 IT recession.
relevance_rationale: Documents how Aberdeen managed through crisis using technology (conferencing, e-learning); directly relevant to business continuity and remote work adoption patterns seen again during COVID-19.
prescience_rationale: Kastner predicted double-digit Q4 2001 spending decline and 2002 recession; both proved accurate as the post-9/11 economic shock compounded the dot-com bust.
